steps.title: Keep title and name together
steps.content: The phrase should read as one noun phrase: Flemish cartographer Abraham Ortelius. The participial phrase that follows, known for showing the world in a cordiform projection, needs a comma before it.

steps.title: Reject misplaced commas
steps.content: Choice B and Choice D wrongly split cartographer from Abraham Ortelius. Choice C keeps the name together but omits the comma before the descriptive phrase. The version with a comma after the name fits. The answer is A.
